$comment: Declarative execution-gate manifest (DATA, not a JSON-Schema document — hence no .schema. in the name). Consumed by scripts/run_gate.py via `validate.sh gate <phase> <run_folder>`. Each phase's entry_requires lists: artifacts that must resolve to a file; mechanical checks (validator + target artifact keys) the gate runs; and attested preconditions (contract-driven / judgment, no validator) the model must confirm — each {id, text} with a STABLE id (Increment 5): a clearing pass records the confirmed ids in gate_events[].attested_items and snapshots the required-id set in attested_contract. Source of truth for execution gating — run-core.md / run-synthesis.md reference it instead of restating the mechanical checklist. See docs/runner-governed-execution.md.

attested.text: all selected Tier-2 passes are complete and appended to the ledger
all auto-run audits (per contract + constraint flags) are complete and in the ledger
every deferred/declined high-risk audit is recorded as an explicit blind spot
each root cause cites >= 1 specific ledger finding (findings integration)
root-cause / revision-checklist / must-fix caps are within limits (cap compliance)
every deferred/declined high-risk audit is named where it limits confidence or readiness (blind-spot disclosure)

entry_requires.$comment_check: A revision_round ENTRY check must be a precondition, not the post-condition the gate establishes. finding-trace W3 (resolved-but-not-yet-revised) would fire here — the gate is what advances revised — so run_checks (WARN-in-output => pass-with-warn) would block the clear. ledger-check is a safe precondition; report<->sidecar consistency is audited by finding-trace AFTER the fold writes revised.

attested.text: delta scan complete — each prior finding sorted into resolved / still-present / new
every confirmed-resolved finding carries an explicit <!-- resolved: <id> --> marker in the revision report
declined items are recorded, not silently re-flagged
